audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra Day of Atonement text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for post-Nadab/Abihu entry boundaries, inner-sanctuary cleansing, two goats, Azazel/removal rite, incense cloud, blood/life rites, self-affliction, Sabbath-rest, and communal atonement.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses after death of two sons of Aaron, when they drew near before YHWH and died.
YHWH said to Moses, Speak to Aaron your brother, that he not come at any time into holy place inside veil, before cover that is upon ark, so he does not die; for in cloud I appear over cover.
With this Aaron shall come into holy place: with bull, son of herd, for sin/purification offering, and ram for burnt offering.

Holy linen tunic he shall put on, linen undergarments shall be on his flesh, linen sash he shall gird, and linen turban he shall wrap; holy garments they are. He shall wash his flesh in water and put them on.
From congregation of children of Israel he shall take two male goats for sin/purification offering and one ram for burnt offering.
Aaron shall bring near bull of sin/purification offering that is for himself, and make atonement/covering for himself and for his house.
He shall take two goats and stand them before YHWH at entrance of tent of meeting.
Aaron shall place lots upon two goats: one lot for YHWH and one lot for Azazel.
Aaron shall bring near the goat upon which lot for YHWH came up, and make it sin/purification offering.
But goat upon which lot for Azazel came up shall be stood alive before YHWH, to make atonement/covering upon it, to send it away to Azazel, to wilderness.

Aaron shall bring near bull of sin/purification offering that is for himself, and make atonement/covering for himself and for his house, and slaughter bull of sin/purification offering that is for himself.
He shall take full firepan of coals of fire from upon altar before YHWH, and full double handfuls of finely ground fragrant incense, and bring inside veil.
He shall put incense on fire before YHWH, and cloud of incense shall cover cover that is upon testimony, so he does not die.
He shall take from blood/life of bull and sprinkle with his finger on face of cover eastward; before cover he shall sprinkle seven times from blood/life with his finger.

He shall slaughter goat of sin/purification offering that is for people, bring its blood/life inside veil, and do with its blood/life as he did with blood/life of bull, sprinkling it upon cover and before cover.

He shall make atonement/covering for holy place from uncleannesses of children of Israel and from their transgressions, for all their sins/misalignments; so he shall do for tent of meeting dwelling with them in midst of their uncleannesses.

No human shall be in tent of meeting when he comes to make atonement/covering in holy place until he comes out, and he has made atonement/covering for himself, for his house, and for all assembly of Israel.
He shall go out to altar that is before YHWH and make atonement/covering upon it; take from blood/life of bull and from blood/life of goat, and put on horns of altar all around.
He shall sprinkle upon it from blood/life with his finger seven times, cleanse it and sanctify it from uncleannesses of children of Israel.
When he has finished making atonement/covering for holy place, tent of meeting, and altar, he shall bring near living goat.

Aaron shall lay two hands on head of living goat and confess over it all iniquities of children of Israel, all their transgressions, for all their sins/misalignments, and put them on head of goat; he shall send it away by hand of appointed man into wilderness.
The goat shall carry upon itself all their iniquities to cut-off land; he shall send goat away in wilderness.
Aaron shall come into tent of meeting, strip off linen garments he put on when he came into holy place, and leave them there.
He shall wash flesh in water in holy place, put on his garments, come out and make his burnt offering and burnt offering of people, and make atonement/covering for himself and for people.
Fat of sin/purification offering he shall turn into smoke on altar.
The one who sent goat to Azazel shall wash garments and wash flesh in water; afterward he may come into camp.

Bull of sin/purification offering and goat of sin/purification offering, whose blood/life was brought to make atonement/covering in holy place, shall be brought outside camp; their skins, flesh, and dung they shall burn in fire.
The one who burns them shall wash garments and wash flesh in water; afterward he may come into camp.
It shall be to you as age-long statute: in seventh month, on tenth of month, you shall afflict/humble your souls/lives and do no work, native and sojourner who sojourns in your midst.
For on this day he shall make atonement/covering for you, to cleanse you; from all your sins/misalignments before YHWH you shall be clean.
It is Sabbath of Sabbath-rest to you, and you shall afflict/humble your souls/lives; age-long statute.
The priest whom they anoint and whose hand they fill to serve as priest in place of his father shall make atonement/covering, and he shall put on linen garments, holy garments.
He shall make atonement/covering for holy sanctuary, for tent of meeting and altar, and for priests and all people of assembly he shall make atonement/covering.
This shall be for you as age-long statute, to make atonement/covering for children of Israel from all their sins/misalignments once in year. And he did as YHWH commanded Moses.

translation_notes: Leviticus 16 is explicitly framed after Nadab and Abihu; entry boundaries are about dangerous nearness to holy presence.
Kippur/atonement includes cleansing sanctuary from accumulated uncleannesses and transgressions, not only individual guilt relief.
Azazel is preserved without over-definition; removal goat carries iniquities away into wilderness/cut-off land.
Two-hand laying and confession over the living goat enact communal burden transfer/removal.
Self-affliction/humbling and no work apply to native and sojourner alike; communal cleansing includes resident outsider.
safety_note: Do not use Leviticus 16 for self-hatred or fear-based religion. The chapter creates a yearly rite for sanctuary cleansing, communal burden removal, rest, humility, and renewed cleanness before YHWH.
